name: Button
description: Toolbar-footprint toggle button. Inactive (urging) = primary fill that invites the commit; active (committed) = surface fill with hairline outline that records the commit without re-soliciting it. Follow ↔ Following, Subscribe ↔ Subscribed, Join ↔ Joined.

active.description: Whether the action has been committed. Maps to aria-pressed.

label.description: Required, single line. Swap between the urging verb ("Follow") and the committed participle ("Following") at the consumer.

leadingIcon.description: Context glyph before the label — most often a check appearing on commit.

trailingIcon.description: Directional / destination glyph after the label.

focusIndicator.description: Keyboard-focus visual — an accessibility indicator, not a lifecycle state. Composes over whichever lifecycle state the button is in. The `states.focused` block above is kept for JSX runtime consumers; this block is the parallel external-reader contract.
focusIndicator.compositionReason: Action affordance with breathing room around it; the 3px outward extent is reserved by the surrounding layout.
focusIndicator.trigger: :focus-visible (keyboard / programmatic focus, never plain mouse click)

forbidden: active state painted with sys.color.primary fill — active is sys.color.surfaceContainerHigh + hairline outline (the active state recedes, not asserts)
rest state without an explicit `selected={false}` — toggle is a binary contract, never tristate
manual width override that breaks the full-card stretch when used inside ProfileCarousel.followAction
